explicit_text: I was born and raised in Lexington, Kentucky, before moving to Mount Sterling in 1971 with my husband. Lexington is where my roots are, and it shaped my early life before I got deeply involved in education and community work in other parts of Kentucky.
implicit_text: I was born and raised in the city known as the Horse Capital of the World, before moving to a smaller town in 1971 with my husband. That city is where my roots are, and it shaped my early life before I got deeply involved in education and community work in other parts of the state.

explicit_text: My husband and I moved to Mount Sterling in 1971, and that's where I really started my long career in education. I stayed involved in the community there for many years, eventually retiring in 2008 before taking on leadership roles in the local education foundation and then The Post Clinic.
implicit_text: My husband and I moved to a small city in eastern Kentucky in 1971, and that's where I really started my long career in education. I stayed involved in the community there for many years, eventually retiring in 2008 before taking on leadership roles in the local education foundation and then the regional clinic.

explicit_text: Montgomery County is where The Post Clinic is based, and since 2011 I've been working there as the director. The clinic originally served Montgomery County and its contiguous counties, and it has grown significantly since then. I also worked with the Montgomery County Education Foundation before joining the clinic.
implicit_text: The county where the regional clinic is based has been my workplace since 2011 as the director. The clinic originally served this county and its contiguous counties, and it has grown significantly since then. I also worked with the local education foundation before joining the clinic.

explicit_text: I have been with The Post Clinic since June 2011, and it has been the most rewarding experience of my life. The clinic started in 1996 serving Montgomery County and nearby counties, and now it serves nearly forty counties including parts of Virginia. We provide medical and dental care to people who often have no insurance or means to pay, and I handle the background work while doctors and nurses care for the patients. The clinic has grown from a cramped space in the old post office to a much larger facility gifted by Dr. Byram Ratliff and his wife Jennifer. The sense of community and the gratitude from patients make this work truly meaningful.
implicit_text: I have been with the regional healthcare facility since June 2011, and it has been the most rewarding experience of my life. It started in 1996 serving the county and nearby counties, and now it serves nearly forty counties including parts of a neighboring state. We provide medical and dental care to people who often have no insurance or means to pay, and I handle the background work while doctors and nurses care for the patients. The clinic has grown from a cramped space in the old post office to a much larger facility gifted by a local doctor and his wife. The sense of community and the gratitude from patients make this work truly meaningful.

explicit_text: At one point, The Post Clinic did not accept Medicare patients because Medicare covers doctor visits and medications, but we made an exception for a very sick patient who only had Medicare Part A, which covers hospital visits. Eventually, the board changed the policy to accept Medicare patients who meet our income guidelines, which is important because some patients have limited coverage and still need our help.
implicit_text: At one point, the clinic did not accept federal health insurance for seniors because it covers doctor visits and medications, but we made an exception for a very sick patient who only had hospital coverage. Eventually, the board changed the policy to accept patients with this insurance who meet our income guidelines, which is important because some patients have limited coverage and still need our help.

explicit_text: We base our income eligibility at The Post Clinic on three-hundred percent of the Federal Poverty Level because that's what pharmaceutical companies require for their Patient Assistance Programs. This threshold helps us serve many patients who can't afford even generic medications, especially given rising costs of living like gas and groceries. It’s a critical benchmark that allows us to help those truly in need.
implicit_text: We base our income eligibility at the clinic on three-hundred percent of the national poverty guideline because that's what pharmaceutical companies require for their Patient Assistance Programs. It’s a critical benchmark that allows us to help those truly in need.

explicit_text: The Post Clinic serves patients all over Eastern Kentucky and beyond, including many counties throughout the state. I have lived and worked in Kentucky most of my life, starting in Lexington and Mount Sterling, and now serving a broad community through the clinic. Kentucky’s rural areas especially benefit from the medical and dental care we provide.
